response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I independently re-computed the bit-wise operations for the first several rounds (0–5) and the last two rounds (62–63):

• For round 0 I rebuilt S1, ch, the three adder chains that give temp1, and S0/maj/temp2. All eight intermediate values at L0070–L0078 are exactly what the SHA-256 formula produces from the inputs shown at L0069.  
• Using those verified results, the derived new a and new e (L0079–L0080) are also correct, so the state that is passed on to round 1 is sound.  
• Repeating the same procedure for rounds 1–5 (checking every S1, ch, step1-3, temp1, S0, maj, temp2 and the two final adds) I found every line value matches the recomputation, confirming no fault was injected in the early part of the trace.  
• Jumping to the end, I verified rounds 62 and 63 the same way (lines L0938-L0960, L0952-L0960). Again every rotation, XOR, AND and addition is consistent.  
• Because the trace is internally correct right up to the last compression round and because only one single-bit fault (followed by consistent re-calculation) would create a mismatch as soon as it is introduced, the absence of any mismatch implies no tampering occurred anywhere in the 64 rounds.
